topic.title: Guest Speaker Participation: A Reality Check
topic.text:
Hi everyone,

I need to be straight with you about today's session with Scott Redick.

Scott gave up his Saturday to share his experience with you. He came prepared, he brought real insights, and only three of you asked questions. That's not okay.

Let me be clear about how participation works going forward: just showing up on camera is not participation. Only the students who actively engaged and asked questions today will receive participation credit. Attendance alone does not count.

As Prof. Nik said, assume everyone has the same credentials you have. The same degree, the same skills, the same resume. What will actually get you a job is networking. When a senior professional takes time out of their weekend to talk to you, that is a networking opportunity. Asking a thoughtful question is how you get noticed. It's how you start a relationship. It's how you stand out.

We have three more guest speakers this semester. Three more chances to make a real connection with someone who could impact your career. Please show up ready to participate. Not just ready to watch.

Professors Nik Bear Brown & Nina
